top_stories.title: Cisco Patches Four Critical Identity Services, Webex Flaws Enabling Code Execution
top_stories.summary: Cisco has released patches for four critical vulnerabilities affecting its Identity Services Engine (ISE) and Webex, with CVSS scores up to 9.8. The flaws include improper certificate validation in SSO integration that could allow attackers to impersonate any user within the service, alongside other vulnerabilities enabling arbitrary code execution.

top_stories.title: Cross‑tenant helpdesk impersonation to data exfiltration: A human-operated intrusion playbook
top_stories.summary: Threat actors are exploiting Microsoft Teams' cross-tenant external collaboration features to impersonate IT helpdesk staff and socially engineer users into granting remote access. The attack chain leverages legitimate admin tools and standard protocols for lateral movement and data exfiltration, making it directly relevant to identity-based attack vectors and access management controls.

top_stories.title: Vishing attacks on Okta identity systems on the rise
top_stories.summary: Vishing attacks targeting Okta identity systems are increasing, with attackers calling victims or IT help desks to socially engineer MFA resets or weakening. LevelBlue researchers detailed the attack methodology in an April 13 blog post, noting that once Okta is compromised through this technique, attackers gain broad access to connected systems.

top_stories.title: Vercel Employee's AI Tool Access Led to Data Breach
top_stories.summary: A Vercel employee's access to an AI tool resulted in a data breach via stolen OAuth tokens. Security researchers highlight OAuth tokens as an emerging primary attack surface for lateral movement in enterprise environments.

top_stories.title: Tycoon 2FA Phishers Scatter, Adopt Device Code Phishing
top_stories.summary: Threat actors behind the Tycoon 2FA phishing kit have shifted tactics to adopt device code phishing, a method that bypasses MFA protections. The attack exploits legitimate OAuth device authorization flows to trick victims into granting account access without stealing passwords directly.

news.title: No Exploit Needed: How Attackers Walk Through the Front Door via Identity-Based Attacks
news.summary: The article examines identity-based attacks as a primary breach vector, focusing on how attackers use stolen credentials rather than technical exploits to gain initial access. It covers credential stuffing and related techniques that bypass traditional security controls by abusing legitimate authentication mechanisms.

news.title: CISA Adds 8 Exploited Flaws to KEV, Sets April-May 2026 Federal Deadlines
news.summary: CISA added eight vulnerabilities to its Known Exploited Vulnerabilities catalog with federal remediation deadlines in April-May 2026. One of the listed flaws is CVE-2023-27351, an improper authentication vulnerability in PaperCut, which has partial relevance to IAM-adjacent authentication weaknesses.

news.title: ‘Scattered Spider’ Member ‘Tylerb’ Pleads Guilty
news.summary: Tyler Robert Buchanan, a 24-year-old British national and senior member of Scattered Spider, pleaded guilty to wire fraud conspiracy and aggravated identity theft. The charges relate to SMS phishing attacks in 2022 that compromised at least a dozen major technology companies and resulted in cryptocurrency theft from investors.

news.title: Microsoft: Teams increasingly abused in helpdesk impersonation attacks
news.summary: Microsoft has issued a warning about threat actors increasingly abusing Microsoft Teams through helpdesk impersonation attacks to gain access to enterprise networks. The attacks leverage legitimate tools for access and lateral movement, exploiting Teams' external collaboration features to bypass traditional security controls.

news.title: Vercel Breach Tied to Context AI Hack Exposes Limited Customer Credentials
news.summary: Vercel disclosed a security breach originating from the compromise of third-party AI tool Context.ai, which was used by a Vercel employee. The attacker leveraged that access to take over the employee's Google Workspace account and gain unauthorized access to certain internal Vercel systems, exposing limited customer credentials.

news.title: Making opportunistic cyberattacks harder by design
news.summary: Microsoft published details on how it secures Dynamics 365 and Power Platform through credential removal, attack surface reduction, and platform engineering practices. The approach focuses on eliminating standing credentials and reducing opportunistic threat vectors as part of Microsoft's secure-by-design engineering methodology.

news.title: Securely connecting on-premises data systems to Amazon Redshift with IAM Roles Anywhere
news.summary: AWS published a guide on using IAM Roles Anywhere to enable secure, private connections between on-premises data systems and Amazon Redshift. The solution eliminates the need for long-lived access keys or public internet exposure by leveraging certificate-based authentication to obtain temporary IAM credentials.

news.title: AWS Managed Microsoft AD is now available on Windows functional level 2016
news.summary: AWS has automatically upgraded all AWS Managed Microsoft AD directories to Windows functional level 2016. The update applies to both existing and new AWS Directory Service for Microsoft AD instances, bringing updated Active Directory functionality to the managed service.

news.title: [Webinar] Eliminate Ghost Identities Before They Expose Your Enterprise Data
news.summary: A webinar is being promoted addressing the management of non-human identities (NHIs) such as service accounts, API tokens, and OAuth grants. The content focuses on unmanaged automated credentials, citing that compromised service accounts and forgotten API keys were responsible for 68% of cloud breaches in 2024.

news.title: Three Microsoft Defender Zero-Days Actively Exploited; Two Still Unpatched
news.summary: Three zero-day vulnerabilities in Microsoft Defender—codenamed BlueHammer, RedSun, and UnDefend—are being actively exploited by threat actors to gain elevated privileges on compromised systems. Huntress is warning that two of the three flaws remain unpatched, with the vulnerabilities disclosed by a researcher known as Chaotic Eclipse.

news.title: Sam Altman's project World looks to scale its human verification empire. First stop: Tinder. | TechCrunch
news.summary: Sam Altman's World project is expanding its Orb-based biometric human verification system through new partnerships, with Tinder being among the first. The initiative focuses on anonymous identity verification to distinguish humans from AI, representing a significant development in decentralized identity and authentication technology.

news.title: Sam Altman's World Teams With Zoom, Tinder to Better Verify Humans in the AI Age
news.summary: Sam Altman's World (formerly Worldcoin) is expanding its iris-scanning biometric identity verification technology through partnerships with Tinder for U.S. human verification and Zoom's Deep Face feature. The integrations use World's biometric proof-of-humanity system to distinguish real humans from AI in digital interactions.

news.title: Sam Altman’s World project launches major upgrade to fight deepfakes and bots
news.summary: Sam Altman's World project has launched a major upgrade aimed at combating deepfakes and bots through its biometric identity verification system. The project uses iris-scanning orbs to establish proof of human identity, positioning it as a digital identity authentication solution.

news.title: Sam Altman’s World releases major protocol upgrade, introduces World ID app
news.summary: Sam Altman's World project has released a major protocol upgrade and introduced a dedicated World ID app focused on digital identity verification. The update targets human authenticity verification in AI-driven environments, using biometric-based identity credentials to distinguish humans from AI online.

news.title: Data Center Logical Security Global Market Forecast, 2026-2032 - Opportunities Driven by Rapid Cloud Adoption, Complex Hybrid Architectures, and Increasing Regulatory Expectations
news.summary: A market forecast report covering the data center logical security market from 2026 to 2032 has been published, citing cloud adoption, hybrid architectures, and regulatory pressures as key drivers. The report includes identity and access management as one of several focus areas within the broader data center logical security market.

news.title: Microsoft's April patch puts Windows domain controllers into reboot loops — third known issue from KB5082063 is affecting Windows Server 2016 through 2025
news.summary: Microsoft's April patch KB5082063 is causing continuous reboot loops on Windows Server domain controllers running versions 2016 through 2025. This is the third known issue from the update and directly impacts enterprise Active Directory infrastructure, which is foundational to identity and access management operations.

news.title: Two-Factor Authentication Breaks Free From the Desktop
news.summary: The article discusses extending two-factor authentication (2FA) beyond traditional desktop/IT environments to address physical world security threats. It focuses on how threat actors bypass security systems outside standard IT contexts and how 2FA can serve as an additional barrier in those scenarios.

news.title: Microsoft, Salesforce Patch AI Agent Data Leak Flaws
news.summary: Microsoft and Salesforce patched prompt injection vulnerabilities in their respective AI agents, Copilot and Agentforce, that could have allowed external attackers to leak sensitive data. The flaws relate to AI agent security rather than core IAM functionality, though they touch on access control boundaries within AI-assisted enterprise tools.

news.title: Privilege Elevation Dominates Massive Microsoft Patch Update
news.summary: Microsoft released a major patch update addressing 165 vulnerabilities, with privilege elevation bugs accounting for more than half of the total. Two zero-day vulnerabilities were included among the elevation-of-privilege flaws patched in the update.

news.title: Russia Hacked Routers to Steal Microsoft Office Tokens
news.summary: Russian military intelligence hackers are exploiting vulnerabilities in older routers to harvest Microsoft Office authentication tokens from over 18,000 networks. The campaign operates without deploying malware, instead silently siphoning authentication tokens to gain unauthorized access to systems.
